gpt-5.6-terra — Balanced — $2.50 / $15 — 1.05M — 128K
Claude Fable 5 is the highest-priced broadly released tier at $10 input and $50 output.
Claude Opus 4.8, released May 28, costs $5 input and $25 output.
Its direct price up to 512K is $0.30 input and $1.20 output.
GLM-5.2, Qwen 3.7, Kimi K2.7 Code, and MiniMax M3 are the current open-model entries."},{"url":"https://divkix.me/blog/ai-models-compared-2026","title":"Best AI Models July 2026: Fable 5, GPT-5.6 Sol, Grok 4.5, SWE-1.7, MiniMax M3","content":"Feb 25, 2026 — GLM 5.2 and K2.7-Code cover raw open capability and value. cost per finished agent task. GPT-5.6 Sol (max) leads Artificial Analysis Coding Agent Index and is joint #1 with Fable 5 on Arena Code Frontend at lower price than Fable.
The part that changed hardest is cost per finished agent task.
Sol, Terra, and Luna are different lanes.

Weights you cannot host are a PDF with a download button."},{"url":"https://fireworks.ai/blog/best-llms-for-coding","title":"Best LLMs for coding: 2026 roundup","content":"Mar 2, 2026 — GPT-5.4 costs half as much on both input and output ($2.50/$15 vs $5/$30 per 1M) and is already available in the Responses and Chat Completions ...
Kimi K2.6 — 47.1 — 256K — 1T / 32B active (MoE) — Workable — Modified MIT — $0.95 (Fireworks) — $4.00 (Fireworks) — View model
GPT-5.4 costs half as much on both input and output ($2.50/$15 vs $5/$30 per 1M) and is already available in the Responses and Chat Completions APIs.
Gemini is proprietary and only available through Google Cloud and Vertex AI.
Sonnet 4.6's output price is 60% of Opus 4.7's ($15 vs $25 per 1M, or 40% less), and it uses the prior tokenizer.
Worth flagging that DeepSeek's comparison baseline is Opus-4.6, not Opus-4.7.
Workloads that depend on scientific-code generation or broad general intelligence should prefer Kimi K2.6; V4-Pro is the better fit where agentic coding composite and Terminal-Bench Hard dominate the eval.
Factual-recall-heavy coding workflows (CLI-arg lookup, API method recall, version-specific syntax) should expect more retrieval gaps than the flagship closed models.
It uses a Dynamic Sparse Attention MoE (GLM_MOE_DSA) with 256 experts and 8 activated per token.
Unlike the lower-tier Qwen3 releases (Qwen3.6-35B-A3B, coder variants), Plus is accessible only through Alibaba Cloud's DashScope / Bailian platform and through partner inference providers including Fireworks.
For fine-tunable Qwen3.6 variants, the 35B-A3B open-weight model is available on Fireworks and supports LoRA and SFT via FireOptimizer.
gpt-oss-120B is OpenAI's first modern open-weights release, shipped August 5, 2025 under Apache 2.0.
Sonnet 3.5 compiled at 62% on the same eval.
There is no single best LLM for coding in 2026."},{"url":"https://www.morphllm.com/best-ai-model-for-coding","title":"Best LLM for Coding (2026): 12 Models Ranked by SWE ...","content":"Jul 1, 2026 — GLM-5.2, cost per solved task. Claude Sonnet 5 is Anthropic's latest Sonnet model, available beginning June 30, 2026. GLM-5.2 is Z.ai's latest flagship coding model for long-horizon agentic development work.
